apps.name: blackjack
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.description: Blackjack 21. Buttons on the phone, cards on the e-ink.
apps.long_description: Single-deck blackjack. Dealer stands on all 17s. Casual mode is just Deal/Hit/Stand with a lifetime W/L/P counter. Flip 'Play for chips' to add a bank (starts at $100), bet selection, and Double Down. State persists across restarts; mid-hand RAM only.

apps.name: hello
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.description: Minimal demo app — push text and it shows on the screen.
apps.long_description: The simplest possible pwn-app. Useful as a template when building your own. Takes a string from the phone and displays it centered on the e-ink screen.

apps.name: maze
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.description: First-person maze crawler — random or daily-seeded mazes.
apps.long_description: Generates a fresh maze on the device (recursive backtracker) and shows it in a Wizardry-style first-person view. Phone is a D-pad (Turn-left, Forward, Back, Turn-right); the e-ink draws the perspective walls plus a fog-of-war minimap that only reveals visited cells and what you can see straight down an open corridor. Two modes: random (new seed every time) and daily (everyone on every device gets the same maze each day). Find the exit in as few steps as the optimum.

apps.name: magic8
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.description: Magic 8-Ball. Tap Shake for an answer.
apps.long_description: Classic Magic 8-Ball. Ask a yes/no question, tap Shake on the phone, and your fortune appears on the e-ink. The answer lives only on the e-ink — the phone is a remote.

apps.name: tide-sun
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.long_description: Shows today's sunrise and sunset times plus the current moon phase. Pulls daily sun data from Open-Meteo; moon phase is computed device-side using a Conway approximation. Phone-side UI is just a Sync control — the e-ink is the display.

apps.name: weather
apps.author: cristian-milea
apps.long_description: Current temperature, feels-like, and weather condition plus a 12-hour precipitation bar chart. Pulls forecast data from Open-Meteo (no API key needed). Phone-side UI is just a Sync control — the e-ink is the display.
